name: Imperial Rolls
recipeIngredient: 7 Or 8 medium shiitake mushrooms, sliced very thin
recipeInstructions:
These crispy rolls are made with Vietnamese rice paper.
If you cannot find these exotic rice paper sheets, use filo dough instead (See Cook's Note). Be patient and practice a bit. Once you get the hang of it, the rest will go smoothly. Use plenty of water when spraying the sheets, roll quickly, keep the sheets covered as you work. A challenge indeed, but well worth the effort. Rice papers and cellophane noodles can be found in any Asian supermarket.
1. Soak the cellophane noodles in warm water for 2 or 3 minutes. Cut into 4-inch lengths and drain.
2. Cook onions, green peppers, garlic, and jalapeno peppers in sesame oil over high heat until wilted. Stir frequently to prevent burning. When vegetables are wilted, add shiitake mushrooms and cook over low heat until soft but not mushy. Place in a large bowl and cool.
3. When the mixture is cool, add all remaining ingredients except rice paper and vegetable oil. Taste and adjust seasonings.
To assemble:
1. Take one sheet of rice paper out of the package, dip in water or spray with plastic spray bottle, and lay on a flat surface.
2. Place about 3 tablespoons of filling on the bottom of the round and roll up, tucking ends in as you go. Seal with more water and set aside, seam side down. Make all rolls this way.
3. Heat about 2 inches of vegetable oil in a large skillet. When oil is hot but not smoking, add rolls in batches and cook until golden brown on all sides. Remove from pan and drain on lint-free towels; do not use paper towels as wrappers will stick.
Cook's Note:
If using filo dough, cut sheets into long rectangles, approximately 6x8 inches. Place filling at the bottom of each piece of filo dough and roll up, tucking in ends as you go. Brush with melted unsalted butter to seal. Cook until golden brown. Cover stack of filo dough with a damp cloth at all times to prevent drying out.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.
